color_palette.rules: STRICT ADHERENCE TO THESE COLORS. No purple/violet, no bright neon colors, no crypto-startup feel. Gradients must be subtle, monochromatic (black to dark gray) ONLY.

typography.primary_font: system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'SF Pro Display', 'Helvetica Neue', sans-serif
typography.mono_font: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', 'Courier New', monospace
typography.rules: Avoid Inter for marketing headers. Use the native Apple system font stack for the ultimate native macOS feel. Large, bold headlines. High readability. Use mono font for code-inspired visual elements.

layout_strategy.spacing: Generous spacing. Use p-8, p-12, py-24, py-32 for sections to give breathing room. Apple's visual rhythm.
layout_strategy.grids: Use the 'Bento' Grid Protocol (Mode A) for the Features section. Asymmetrical layouts, mixed spans. Use gap-6 or gap-8.
layout_strategy.alignment: Center-align hero and major section headers for impact, but left-align dense feature descriptions or bento content.
layout_strategy.surfaces: Tasteful glassmorphism. Use backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/[0.02] border border-white/[0.08] for premium cards and mockups.

motion_and_interactions.scroll: Fade-up on scroll for all major elements. Staggered feature reveals.
motion_and_interactions.hero: Hero mockup floating subtly using framer-motion (e.g., animate y: [0, -10, 0] with long duration and easeInOut).
motion_and_interactions.hover: Subtle scale-up (scale-105 or scale-[1.02]) on cards, slight border brightness increase on hover (border-white/20).
motion_and_interactions.transitions: Smooth, slow transitions (duration-500 or duration-700 for large elements). Apple product pages use very deliberate, unhurried animations.

images.description: Dark and moody abstract image with soft gradient blur to use behind the hero section with low opacity (10-20%) and mix-blend mode.

icons.style: Minimalist, thin stroke, white or secondary color.

component_instructions.hero_mockup: CRITICAL: Do not use a static image for the hero mockup. Build a native-looking macOS settings window and a menu bar utility using HTML/Tailwind. Use backdrop-blur-2xl, border-white/[0.08], rounded-xl, and macOS window controls (red/yellow/green dots). Layer them side-by-side or overlapping using absolute positioning and framer-motion for a floating effect.
component_instructions.problem_section: Create a visual comparison component. Left side: macOS default (cluttered open apps in dock/background). Right side: Nix behavior (clean, apps actually quit). Use simple, high-contrast visual cues.
component_instructions.how_it_works: Three animated cards (Detect, Decide, Act). Use Framer Motion for sequential fade-ins. Each card should have a premium icon and minimal text.
component_instructions.features_grid: 8-card bento grid. Vary the sizes (e.g., some col-span-2, some col-span-1). Use subtle linear gradients in the background of cards (e.g., from-white/[0.05] to-transparent) and pure white icons.
component_instructions.comparison_table: Build a clean, minimalist comparison table (Nix vs Alternatives). Use checkmarks/crosses. Keep borders very subtle (border-white/[0.05]).
component_instructions.tech_showcase: Built for macOS section. Use a dark code snippet block (bg-[#111111]) with monospaced font highlighting 'SwiftUI', 'AppKit', 'AXObserver'. Add subtle glowing borders.
component_instructions.privacy: Emphasize zero telemetry. Huge typography. 'No analytics. No telemetry. No tracking.' with a striking lock or shield icon.
component_instructions.buttons: Primary CTA: bg-white text-black hover:bg-gray-200 rounded-full px-6 py-3 font-medium transition-all. Secondary CTA: bg-transparent text-white border border-white/[0.2] hover:bg-white/[0.05] rounded-full px-6 py-3 font-medium.

instructions_to_main_agent: You are building a premium macOS app landing page. It must feel like Apple designed it.
Strictly use the provided color palette. ANY use of colors outside black, white, and defined grays is an automatic failure.
Use framer-motion extensively for scroll reveals (fade up) and subtle micro-interactions.

Build the hero macOS mockup entirely with Tailwind UI elements. Make it look like a real macOS settings pane. Use the exact macOS traffic light colors (Red: #FF5F56, Yellow: #FFBD2E, Green: #27C93F) for the window controls to sell the native feel.
All interactive elements MUST have data-testid attributes.
Ensure the layout is perfectly responsive. The hero mockup should stack gracefully on mobile.
